---
title: "Welcome (and a Quick Word on Counterfactuals)"
description: "Kicking off this blog with a short note on why the counterfactual is always estimated, never observed."
date: 2026-06-13
categories: [causal-inference, meta]
---

This is the first post on the blog. Every new post lives in its own folder under `posts/` as an `index.qmd` file -- delete this one once you've got the hang of things, or keep it around as a template.

To make the code-execution side of things concrete, here's a small R/ggplot2 example of a theme that comes up a lot in causal inference: for any single unit, we only ever observe *one* of the potential outcomes (treated or untreated). The other one -- the counterfactual -- has to be estimated.

```{r}
#| label: fig-counterfactual
#| fig-cap: "Observed outcome vs. an estimated counterfactual for a single unit"
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4

library(ggplot2)

set.seed(42)

df <- data.frame(
  time = rep(1:10, 2),
  outcome = c(
    1:5 + rnorm(5, 0, 0.15), 6.8, 7.6, 8.5, 9.3, 10.2,           # observed (treated)
    1:5 + rnorm(5, 0, 0.15), 5.4, 5.8, 6.1, 6.5, 6.9             # counterfactual (estimated)
  ),
  series = rep(c("Observed (treated)", "Counterfactual (estimated)"), each = 10)
)

ggplot(df, aes(time, outcome, color = series, linetype = series)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 5, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey60") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#3a6b8a", "#b0bcc4")) +
  labs(
    x = "Time",
    y = "Outcome",
    color = NULL,
    linetype = NULL,
    title = "The counterfactual is estimated, never observed"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

The dotted line marks the point where treatment begins. Everything to the right of it on the grey line didn't happen -- it's a model's best guess at what *would* have happened. Useful framing to keep handy whenever someone describes a counterfactual as something you can simply "look up."

A few notes on how this project is set up, for future-you:

- `execute: freeze: auto` in `_quarto.yml` means Quarto caches the result of running this code chunk. Re-renders only re-run it if this file changes, and the cached result is checked into `_freeze/` so CI can build the site without R installed.
- `code-fold: true` (set globally) is why the code above is collapsed by default -- click to expand it.
- Add more posts by creating a new folder under `posts/` with its own `index.qmd`.
